decisions.reasoning:
* Technical Analysis: Broad market (SPY/QQQ) is in a healthy uptrend with RSI in the 45-57 range (not overbought). Among single names, the strongest trend setups — AAPL (RSI 61.9, +8.1% 20d), JPM (RSI 62.2, +8.8% 20d), and META (RSI 68.7, +17.2% 20d) — are all trading within ~2-5% of their 20-day highs, meaning fresh long entries now would be chasing an already-extended move rather than entering with room to run. META in particular is overbought (RSI 68.7) and up nearly 15% in 5 days alone. GOOGL, MSFT, and XLE are in mild downtrends (all below their 50-day averages) and do not offer a clean long setup either. NVDA and AVGO show strong 5-day momentum but sit close to their own 20-day highs.

* Fundamental Analysis: No earnings, financial statements, or company-specific news were provided for this decision — per the data constraints, only price/volume/technical data is available today.

* Market Context: This is the first decision for this $1000 paper account. Data reflects the most recently completed session (Friday, 2026-07-10); no new session has occurred since (decision run on a non-trading day).

* Risk Assessment: None of the available setups offer a favorable risk/reward for a fresh entry today — the trending names are extended near recent highs (poor reward for the risk of a pullback), and the laggards are in downtrends without a clear reversal signal. Initiating a position here would be chasing strength rather than a high-probability, well-timed entry. Confidence for any single new trade is below the 50 threshold, so per the decision philosophy (protect capital first, HOLD is often correct, cash is a valid position), the correct call is to hold 100% cash and re-evaluate with fresh data on the next trading session, watching for pullbacks toward the 20-day moving averages in AAPL, JPM, or NVDA as potential lower-risk entries.

* Technical Analysis (Yahoo Finance): Price/technical data has not changed since the last decision — the most recently completed session is still Friday 2026-07-10 (it is currently early Monday, before this session's close). Among the names surveyed: META is extended well above its own 20-day Bollinger upper band (RSI 68.7, +17.2% over 20 days, +14.8% in just the last 5), JPM is testing 20-day-range resistance near $343 heading into known binary catalysts, and NVDA/AVGO/AAPL show constructive but not decisive setups (neutral-to-bullish RSI, mixed MACD crossovers).

* Fundamental Analysis (SEC EDGAR, latest filed quarters): Fundamentals broadly remain strong and unchanged from the last check — NVDA (+85.2% revenue YoY, +210.6% net income YoY), GOOGL (+81.2% net income YoY), AMZN (+76.7% net income YoY) all show robust growth. None of this is new information driving today's call; strong fundamentals alone do not override the near-term technical/event risk below.

* Market Context (Google News headlines, overnight): The dominant new information since Friday is geopolitical, not economic — headlines report "Dow Logs Worst Day Since March... After US-Iran Exchange Fire" hitting the S&P 500 and Nasdaq and snapping their win streak, plus oil-market warnings of a prolonged Middle East conflict pushing energy prices higher. Separately, JPM heads into Q2 earnings and a CPI print this week (binary, gap-risk events a stop-loss cannot protect against), and META's rally is being framed by some coverage as already fully priced ("cash-gushing ad giant... limited diversification").

* Risk Assessment — Bull case: NVDA's valuation reset ("hasn't been this cheap since before 2019" per coverage) plus a forming bullish MACD crossover could argue for an early-stage long. META's AI-driven momentum could continue near-term. Bear case: a fresh geopolitical shock just increased broad-market tail risk overnight; META and JPM are both extended into resistance/highs with binary catalysts imminent (JPM: earnings + CPI this week; META: a rally several sell-side desks are now calling fully priced); NVDA's setup is still unconfirmed (MACD line remains below zero) rather than a clean breakout. Risk-manager check: given the fresh macro shock and the fact that every live candidate is either extended, event-risk-heavy, or unconfirmed, no candidate clears the bar for a favorable, well-timed entry today — the bear case wins on balance. Confidence for any new trade is below the 50 threshold, so per the decision philosophy (protect capital first, cash is a valid position), the correct call is to hold 100% cash again and reassess once Monday's session actually closes and the CPI/earnings catalysts have passed.

* Technical Analyst: AAPL closed $317.31 (+0.63% 1d), above both SMA20 ($299.19) and SMA50 ($298.71), with a bullish MACD crossover (5.24 vs signal 2.57, rising histogram) and RSI14 at 63.7 (strong, not yet overbought — vs META 69.0, AMZN 67.8). Testing its own 20-day high ($323.45) on above-average volume (43.2M vs 64.6M avg20d — actually below average, so this is a price move without a volume surge to confirm it).
* Fundamentals Analyst: SEC EDGAR, quarter ending 2026-03-28 — revenue +16.60% YoY, net income +19.36% YoY, diluted EPS $2.01. Healthy, accelerating growth; not a name trading purely on momentum.
* News/Sentiment Analyst (Google News, single-day sample, treat as directional not confirmed): analyst price-target hike to $365; "AAPL, QTTB, CSX stocks surge to 52-week highs"; coverage frames this as capital rotating into AAPL specifically as an "AI caution" beneficiary, while Meta/Amazon drew skeptical coverage over rising AI capex and NVDA/AVGO/TSLA all sold off the same day.
* Bull Case: confirmed uptrend, fresh bullish MACD crossover, a real upside catalyst (analyst target well above the current 20-day high), accelerating fundamentals, and relative strength versus both a weak broad tape (SPY -0.77%, QQQ -1.90%) and a selling-off AI-hardware cohort — this reads as idiosyncratic strength, not a beta trade.
* Bear Case: RSI already elevated with the 20-day high as immediate overhead resistance; the volume behind the move is unremarkable (below its own 20-day average), so the breakout is not yet volume-confirmed; broader geopolitical tail risk (Iran-linked selloff) has eased but not fully cleared, and a market-wide risk-off leg could still drag AAPL down regardless of its own relative strength.
* Trader Synthesis: bull case wins on balance — the combination of trend, catalyst, and relative strength outweighs the lack of volume confirmation — but conviction is moderate (64/100), not high, given the unconfirmed volume and lingering macro tail risk.
* Risk Manager Sign-off: APPROVED — sized to exactly 2% of equity risk (stop $308, ~2.9% below entry, just under the recent swing area), risk/reward approximately 1:2.2, well within the 3% daily loss limit, and this is the only position open today so there is no correlation/concentration concern to flag.

[Data-source note: this session's network egress policy again blocked the structured pipeline (query1.finance.yahoo.com, news.google.com, www.sec.gov all returned policy-level 403s at the proxy), and a WebFetch retry got a direct 403 from Yahoo itself. Current price/news context below came from web search instead — real but qualitative, with noticeably lower precision than the usual OHLCV/technical-indicator/EDGAR feed (e.g. two different NVDA 'current' prices appeared within a single search result). No new position was sized off this data as a result; the existing AAPL position is being evaluated only against its own already-approved stop/target plan, which does not require fresh numeric precision to check.]

* Technical Analyst: The open AAPL long (2.15 sh @ avg $317.47, opened 2026-07-14) remains between its stop ($308) and take-profit ($338) — web search puts AAPL around a fresh record high (~$321 on 2026-07-13) and still elevated into 2026-07-14 news, so neither exit level has been touched and no action on this position is required. Among other names, NVDA/JPM/AVGO show continuing momentum per news coverage, but with the numeric pipeline unreachable there is no way to verify a precise fresh technical setup for any of them beyond the same qualitative search data.
* Fundamentals Analyst: JPM (Q2 2026 beat: $16.9B net income, $6.14 EPS) and MSFT (FQ2 2026 beat: $81.3B revenue +17% YoY, EPS $4.14) both already popped on their prints per web search — the fundamental positive is real but already reflected in price. AMZN and META continue to carry flagged 2026 AI capex guidance ($125-145B range cited for META) pressuring near-term free cash flow, unchanged from the last check.
* News/Sentiment Analyst (web search aggregation, not the usual Google News RSS per-ticker feed — treat as directional only): broad tape described as choppy on Strait of Hormuz/Iran-related oil headlines, partially offset by a cooler-than-expected June CPI print; nothing ticker-specific enough to justify fresh position risk today.
* Bull Case: AAPL's post-Broadcom-deal, CEO-transition narrative (Citi target raised to $365) argues for staying the course on the existing long rather than trimming early; JPM/NVDA/AVGO momentum could continue.
* Bear Case: every named momentum candidate (AAPL, JPM, NVDA, AVGO) had already made its move by the time this session could act — chasing any of them now means buying strength rather than a fresh, favorable entry, and this session's degraded data feed alone fails the bar for computing an auditable stop-loss/risk-reward on any *new* position regardless of directional view.
* Trader Synthesis: no ticker earns a fresh entry today; the existing AAPL long stays open exactly as originally sized, unchanged.
* Risk Manager Sign-off: VETOED for any new trade today — position-sizing/stop-loss math cannot be computed to the required precision without the structured OHLCV/technical-indicator pipeline (blocked again this session). APPROVED (no-op) for continuing to hold the existing AAPL position unchanged, since it remains within its original, already-risk-managed stop/target band.
decisions._dataAsOf: 2026-07-14 (web search fallback — structured pipeline unreachable this session, see reasoning)

decisions.reasoning:
[Data-source note: the structured pipeline (query1.finance.yahoo.com, news.google.com, www.sec.gov) was unreachable again this session (policy-level 403s), so web search was used for qualitative price/news context instead — real but imprecise, same caveat as 2026-07-15.]

* Technical Analyst: 2026-07-15 was a broad, single-driver rally day — AAPL +4% to a record $327.50 (+20% YTD), MSFT +3.19% to $395.63 (recovering from the 07-14 IBM-driven enterprise-software selloff), AMZN +3.0% to $254.96, META +3.1% to $681.31 (+17% in July alone), JPM +1.17% to $346.91 (record Q2, nearing $1T market cap), and the S&P 500/Nasdaq-100 both rose (+0.38% / +0.64%) — nearly every mega-cap name jumped 3%+ in the same session on the same catalyst (cooler June CPI, 3.5% YoY vs 3.8% expected, easing rate-hike fears) rather than idiosyncratic strength. NVDA (+0.3% to ~$211.81) and AVGO (+1.3% to ~$394) moved far less despite strong fundamentals (NVDA: 85% revenue growth, $80B buyback authorized), suggesting those two are not part of the same extended move. XLE pulled back (~$55.99 from $56.95) on a US-Iran peace deal easing the oil supply-crunch narrative. The existing AAPL long (2.15 sh @ avg $317.47, opened 2026-07-14, stop $308 / target $338) is now sitting on a solid unrealized gain (~$327.50 mark) but has not hit its take-profit, so no action is needed on it.
* Fundamentals Analyst: JPM's Q2 print was genuinely strong (record earnings, $16.9B net income, +27% YoY revenue, multiple analyst target hikes) and NVDA's reported 85% YoY revenue growth plus an $80B buyback authorization are real positives — but both are already fully reflected in yesterday's price action, so buying today means paying for information the market has already priced in.
* News/Sentiment Analyst (web search aggregation, not the usual Google News RSS per-ticker feed — treat as directional only): the dominant story is macro (cooler CPI, rate-cut hopes) rather than stock-specific, which is exactly the kind of single-factor, market-wide move the Risk Manager should treat with extra caution — several 'independent' candidates today would really just be one correlated bet on the same catalyst. AAPL's China Apple-Intelligence approval (Alibaba's Qwen model) is a genuine idiosyncratic positive already reflected in its price. GOOGL carries added headline risk (EU antitrust fine reportedly being prepared, a DeepMind researcher resignation over an AI-military-deal controversy).
* Bull Case: JPM, NVDA, and AVGO all have real, still-developing fundamental catalysts (record earnings, buyback, insatiable AI-chip demand per AVGO's CEO) that could argue for a fresh entry on any pullback; the existing AAPL position could be added to given its strong trend continuation.
* Bear Case: every headline mover from 07-15 (AAPL, MSFT, AMZN, META, JPM) already made an outsized one-day move on a shared macro catalyst — chasing any of them today means buying an extended, correlated spike rather than a fresh, well-timed, diversified entry; this is compounded by this session's degraded (web-search-only) data, which can't support the precise stop-loss/risk-reward math the Risk Manager requires for a brand-new position. Adding to the existing AAPL long into a fresh record high, on the same day it's already up sharply, would also be chasing rather than following the original plan.
* Trader Synthesis: no ticker earns a fresh entry today, and the existing AAPL long is left exactly as originally sized — it's working as intended and doesn't need to be touched just because it's green.
* Risk Manager Sign-off: VETOED for any new trade today — both the correlation/concentration concern (nearly every live candidate moved on the same macro driver yesterday) and the degraded data precision independently fail the sign-off bar. APPROVED (no-op) for continuing to hold the existing AAPL position unchanged, since it remains within its original stop/target band and is performing as planned.
